title: Sree Poornathrayeesa Temple
tagline: Sree Poornathrayeesa Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Sree Poornathrayesa temple (in Malayalam: ശ്രീ പൂര്‍ണ്ണത്രയീശ ക്ഷേത്രം) is a Hindu temple situated in Thrippunithura, Kochi, the capital of the former Kingdom of Cochin, Kerala, In
overview.description: Sree Poornathrayesa temple (in Malayalam: ശ്രീ പൂര്‍ണ്ണത്രയീശ ക്ഷേത്രം) is a Hindu temple situated in Thrippunithura, Kochi, the capital of the former Kingdom of Cochin, Kerala, India. The temple is considered among the greatest temples in Kerala and was the first among eight royal temples of the erstwhile Kingdom of Cochin. The presiding deity is Vishnu as Santhanagopala Murthy or Poornathrayeesa. He was the national deity of Cochin and protector guardian of Tripunithura.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Iron Bridge (Thrippunithura)
topPlaces.description: Irumbu Palam (Iron Bridge) in Thrippunithura, Kerala is one of the earliest iron bridges in India. The bridge was built across the Champakara canal (part of National Waterway 3) connecting Tripunithura and Poonithura which is part of the present Kochi Municipal Corporation.

topPlaces.name: Chakkamkulangara Siva Temple
topPlaces.description: Chakkamkulangara Siva Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shiva and Devi Parvathi is situated at Thrippunithura of Ernakulam District in Kerala state in India. The Chakkamkulangara temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva however temple is equally famous for the Navagraha pratishta.

topPlaces.name: The Sanctuary (Kochi)
topPlaces.description: The Sanctuary is the training ground and academy base of the Indian football club Kerala Blasters FC. The club officially announced the completion of development on 5 September 2025. Located in Thrippunithura (Kochi, Kerala) to the east of Kochi and north of Thrippunithura Mini Bypass Road, and covering 7140 m2, it has been used for training of senior team, academy, and reserves since 2025.

topPlaces.name: Thrippunithura
topPlaces.description: Thrippunithura or Tripunithura (Malayalam:തൃപ്പൂണിത്തുറ) is a prominent historical and residential region in the city of Kochi in Kerala, India. Located about 7 km (4 mi) from the city centre, Tripunithura was the capital of the erstwhile Kingdom of Cochin.

topPlaces.name: Thamaramkulangara Sree Dharma Sastha Temple
topPlaces.description: Thamaramkulangara Sree Dharma Sastha temple (Malayalam: താമരംകുളങ്ങര ശ്രി ധര്‍മശാസ്താ ക്ഷേത്രം) is a temple in Tripunithura, Kochi, in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Vadakkekotta metro station
topPlaces.description: Vadakkekotta metro station is a metro station of Kochi Metro in India. It was opened on 1 September 2022 as a part of the extension of the metro system from Petta to SN Junction.

topPlaces.name: SN Junction metro station
topPlaces.description: SN Junction metro station is a metro station of Kochi Metro. It was opened on 1 September 2022 as a part of the extension of the metro system from Pettah to SN Junction.

topPlaces.name: Thrippunithura Terminal metro station
topPlaces.description: Thrippunithura Terminal is a terminal metro station serving as the southern terminal of the Kochi Metro which was opened on 6 March 2024. It was inaugurated by the Prime Minister of India Narendra Modi on 6 March 2024 and marked the completion of final station of the first phase of the Kochi metro system that starts from Aluva.

items.note: Irumbu Palam (Iron Bridge) in Thrippunithura, Kerala is one of the earliest iron bridges in India. The bridge was built across the Champakara canal (part of National Waterway 3) connecting Tripunithura and Poonithura which is part of the present Kochi Municipal Corporation.

Chakkamkulangara Siva Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shiva and Devi Parvathi is situated at Thrippunithura of Ernakulam District in Kerala state in India. The Chakkamkulangara temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva however temple is equally famous for the Navagraha pratishta.

The Sanctuary is the training ground and academy base of the Indian football club Kerala Blasters FC. The club officially announced the completion of development on 5 September 2025. Located in Thrippunithura (Kochi, Kerala) to the east of Kochi and north of Thrippunithura Mini Bypass Road, and covering 7140 m2, it has been used for training of senior team, academy, and reserves since 2025.

Thrippunithura or Tripunithura (Malayalam:തൃപ്പൂണിത്തുറ) is a prominent historical and residential region in the city of Kochi in Kerala, India. Located about 7 km (4 mi) from the city centre, Tripunithura was the capital of the erstwhile Kingdom of Cochin.
Thamaramkulangara Sree Dharma Sastha temple (Malayalam: താമരംകുളങ്ങര ശ്രി ധര്‍മശാസ്താ ക്ഷേത്രം) is a temple in Tripunithura, Kochi, in the state of Kerala, India.
Vadakkekotta metro station is a metro station of Kochi Metro in India. It was opened on 1 September 2022 as a part of the extension of the metro system from Petta to SN Junction.

itinerary.title: Day 3 — SN Junction metro station & around

items.note: SN Junction metro station is a metro station of Kochi Metro. It was opened on 1 September 2022 as a part of the extension of the metro system from Pettah to SN Junction.

Thrippunithura Terminal is a terminal metro station serving as the southern terminal of the Kochi Metro which was opened on 6 March 2024. It was inaugurated by the Prime Minister of India Narendra Modi on 6 March 2024 and marked the completion of final station of the first phase of the Kochi metro system that starts from Aluva.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sree Poornathrayeesa Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sree Poornathrayeesa Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sree Poornathrayeesa Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (24 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (7 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sree Poornathrayeesa Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sree Poornathrayeesa Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sree Poornathrayeesa Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Sree Poornathrayeesa Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sree Poornathrayeesa Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Iron Bridge (Thrippunithura), Chakkamkulangara Siva Temple, The Sanctuary (Kochi), Thrippunithura, Thamaramkulangara Sree Dharma Sastha Temple.
